macro_segments.title: The Metzora-Baal Keri Case: How Can Thumbs Enter if Tamei Cannot?

macro_segments.title: Answers to the Bia Bimiktzat Dilemma: Hutrah vs. Dchuya Framework

micro_segments.title: Reish Lakish's Answer: Metzora Has Special Dispensation; Applies to Ba'al Keri Status Too
Rav Yosef's Inference: If Dispension Works This Way, Robam Zavim Rules Logically

Gemara Reconsiders: Reverse Order (Tmei Meis Then Zav) Works Better Logically
Gemara Final Challenge: Metzora Hutrah (No Problem) vs. Tumah Dchuya (Overridden)
Rava's Rebuttal: Dchuya Means Override, So More Things Can Be Overridden Together
Conclusion: Bia Bimiktzat Either Not a Problem or Special Metzora Heter

macro_segments.title: Transition: Agreement on Metzora Hutrah and Tumah Dchuya for Tomorrow's Discussion

micro_segments.title: Both Amoraim Agree: Metzora Is Hutrah (No Problem); Tumah Is Dchuya (Overridden)
